sections.title: Before being teleported to Harmony Island

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Excuse me...oh, it's you.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Have you thought any more about helping our order?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, I might have done. What was the problem again?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Our island has been overrun by undead pirates.

sections.title: After being teleported to Harmony Island

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: So, have you found anything out about the pirates' plans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing yet... What would you recommend doing again?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Well, the secret passage into the monastery is located in the gardens.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: If you pull the statue there out of the way you'll be able to get into the tunnels, and from there into the monastery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Talking to the monks inside the mill
Talking to the Zombie monks on the main or top floor

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: What if I start craving brains?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just eat a cauliflower or something!

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: I don't even know what one of those is!

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: This is so awful...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang in there, brother, [player name] is on the case.

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: Something fell off! Something fell off!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't wanna know!

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: I wish I was back in my own body.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it, okay? Give me a minute.

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: I can feel my brain thumping about in here when I shake my head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then try to stay still!

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: I don't want to be stuck this way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You won't be, trust me!

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: This body feels itchy...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Likely that's all in the mind...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry! Sorry!

sections.title: Talking to the monks on the main floor

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I suppose I should view this as a test of faith...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the spirit!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Except the holy texts are light on plagues of zombie pirates.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Zombies! What ever will we do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Keep calm. I'm a professional. I'll take care of it.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I knew I should have been a bricklayer...

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I don't want to lose my brain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You won't, trust me!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I was almost all the way through translating that book too...

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: What if they get in while I'm asleep? Oh no!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: So, have you dealt with this sort of problem before?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, although the zombies were drunker, the hostages were less holy and the island was known for exporting swill, not piety.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Oh, well that's okay then.

sections.title: Talking to the monks or Brother Tranquility below the mill

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Glabburbleglubble...

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Blubblelubblebubblelubble...

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Plib!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Blublublubluh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, without a brain, these monks seem a lot less holy all of a sudden...

sections.title: Talking to the zombie monks on the top floor

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: I don't want to be stuck this way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You won't be, trust me!

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: Something fell off! Something fell off!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't wanna know!

sections.title: Exploring the island before investigating the statue
Searching the northern ship too early
Searching the chest to the west
Taking a keg or tinderbox from the upper floor
Knocking on the temple's doors too early
Interacting with the stairs while underwater
Sneaking past the tunnel into the library

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* I have received a summons to attend to the Captain and so will be 'down below' for a while.

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* How is the gas production progressing?

lines.speaker: Sorebones
lines.text: Arr, it be progressin' well...

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* What is it? I can smell your lie, whelp.

lines.speaker: Sorebones
lines.text: No, no! 'Tis just that we need more ingredients fer enough te blanket the island!

lines.speaker: Sorebones
lines.text: If ye'd just let us storm the granary we'd be able te take them monks out real easy like.

lines.speaker: Sorebones
lines.text: Why, that thing of yers could hammer the wall down...

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze!* Silence! I will not be questioned by underlings!

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* If you or the others do not consider yourselves capable of carrying out Rabid Jack's orders, then perhaps you need to spend some time in 'The Box'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rabid Jack! That name again...

lines.speaker: Sorebones
lines.text: Please, no! Not 'The Box'!

lines.speaker: Sorebones
lines.text: The sleeping gas will be ready as planned, Surgeon General. Those monks will be takin' a nap for a week when they get a whiff of it, and the experiments will not stand a chance.

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* Excellent...the gas will leave the brain intact for the next stage.

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* This time there will be no rebellion amongst the subjects.

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* Rabid Jack will have his crew of enhanced zombies and I will have as many test subjects as I can vivisect!

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* Hekhekhekhek!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sleeping gas! I have to tell the monks about this right now! I only hope I have time...

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes? Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Sorry to have bothered you; I'm looking for a more experienced adventurer.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: I'm looking for a bold and true adventurer to help me with a...a...a little problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you've found one... Say, you don't look much like a pirate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In fact, you look a little ill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A little more than ill, in fact...

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Shhh!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: That's all part of the problem...and we can't talk here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is starting to sound a little bit dodgy, and I do appreciate I'm in a hive of scum and villainy as I say this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any part of your problem you can tell me?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Well, all I can say is that I am from a small, cloistered order of monks from a tiny island nearby, and that we have a problem with undead pirates...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Undead pirates? Let me at em!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Oh, you'll help? Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Now, I'll just transport us to the island...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No hitting! No hitting!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Are you all right, my child?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just get this thumping pain in the back of my head whenever I think about travelling to an island full of undead pirates.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no idea why.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Hmm, that is odd. Have you considered seeing a doctor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but I might well do soon if it keeps up.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Anyway, I'll just use the teleport spell I created. Let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zombies! Back away from those monks now!

lines.speaker: Zombie monk
lines.text: Wait! Wait! We ARE monks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Seriously? Zombie monks?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Honestly, they are with us, and they aren't zombies.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Sort of, at any rate.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: It's true. We need your help...will you at least listen to us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, but the first one of you that lunges for my brain will be strung up and beaten until they are dead...or very, very sorry.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Oh, but of course. I understand that this is very confusing.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: We are a cloistered order of Saradominist monks. We use this island's relative isolation to translate and illuminate books. We even have a treaty with the pirates; they leave us alone and we provide the occasional

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: blessing they might need.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: This morning, however, we were awakened by the sound of a ship running aground and, as we readied ourselves to venture out to look for survivors, zombie pirates poured into the monastery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were they particularly drunken-looking?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Not that we could tell. They seemed to be being given orders by a twisted little zombie called Mi-Gor and his cadre of sorebones.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Some of us, like myself, were dragged away.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: I remember a terrible pain, and then woke up in this body with Mi-Gor nearby, gloating about having 'enhanced' a zombie with a non-rotting brain.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: I laid low, pretending to still be unconscious and, after a while, made my move, freeing the others that had been 'enhanced'.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: We fought our way to the granary and have barred the doors. We stored our now brainless bodies downstairs in case we can be put back to normal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what have they done as a result?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: It looked like they were going to storm the doors, but then Mi-Gor called them off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Called them off? Any idea why?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: No, none whatsoever.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Now that you know our plight, will you help us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Saradomin be praised!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: There is a secret tunnel into the monastery under the statue in the garden. If you get inside, you can find out what they are planning and we can counter it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well good luck with that.

sections.title: Talking to Brother Tranquility at Mos Le'Harmless

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Do you have the book?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Oh, well hurry to the Edgeville Monastery and get it. We haven't got a moment to lose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, in my bank.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: What use is it there? Go get it and hurry back. We haven't got a moment to lose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have your book here.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: When you took the book from the shelf, did you say the words?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Words? What words?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: The words! The words of power that would prevent...

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Wait a moment, I forgot to tell you them, didn't I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! There were no words! What were they supposed to prevent?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Oh dear...in all the excitement I completely forgot about them...

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: I do hope that we can fix things before the locusts arrive...

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Silly me, all this excitement - and likely that crude brain-operation - made me totally forget. I'm sure that it'll all work out somehow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: LOCUSTS?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Oh, don't worry, it'll only be a little swarm.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Now, back to business.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: So have you read the book?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquillity
lines.text: Well, you should give it a try now, just in case there is anything else you need.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquillity
lines.text: No point in going all the way to Harmony and then back because you need something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have read the prayer and I know how to perform it.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Great! Do you have everything you'll need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I need to go and get a holy symbol.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Well hurry! Who knows what depredations are going on back on Harmony!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have the book and the holy symbol I need.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquillity
lines.text: Excellent! Back to Harmony!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquillity
lines.text: Quickly, the pirates have released the gas! Use the prayer. The other brothers will help make it powerful enough to cover the whole of the granary.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: So, have you found anything out about the pirates' plans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The pirates are planning on using some sort of sleeping gas to knock you all out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When everyone is unconscious, they are going to come in and take you away for brain-swaps!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Fiendish!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: We have to prepare our defence against this threat. I recall something that might well help...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it some sort of potion to counteract the poison?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: No, no. You see, the gas will work on us faster than a normal poison, and a potion would take too long to diffuse through the stomach.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: No, what I'm thinking of is a prayer written centuries ago by one of the abbots at the Edgeville Monastery.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: protected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, how come I've never heard of this prayer?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Well, the abbot was slain by a group of raiders. Apparently his faith was little protection against their repeated sword blows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ouch!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Anyway, the book of prayers he wrote was stored in the Edgeville Monastery, so you will need to go there and retrieve it. And quickly!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Here, I'll take you straight back to Mos Le'Harmless.

sections.title: Interacting with the book of prayers
At Harmony Island, cursed with poison gas

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Quickly, the pirates have released the gas! Use the prayer, the other brothers will help make it powerful enough to cover the whole of the granary.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Are you alright? We dragged you back inside as quickly as we could.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Woah...that gas is pretty potent... I'll need to wear my diving gear outside so that I don't get knocked out again.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: So long as you are unharmed, that is the main thing.

sections.title: Talking to Brother Tranquility after reciting the prayer

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: What did the doctor say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't been to see him yet.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Well, when you do, try your best to persuade him to help.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Want me to take you back to Mos Le'Harmless?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Well done! The power of Saradomin will keep the poison from affecting anyone within these walls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now? Want me to go and kill the zombies?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Well, fun as that would be, I was wondering if you could help us get our brains back into the right bodies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, that was what I was going to suggest next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you want me to do, scrub up and grab a pair of pliers or something?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: No. Just...no.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: While I trust your skills in all other areas, I can state without fear of contradiction when I say that you are not a brain surgeon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: No, what we want you to do is bring Doctor Fenkenstrain to us to do the operation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That evil, demented, eye-rollingly insane butcher? What could you want with him and his twisted...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...brain transplanting...

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: In addition to wanting him to come here and help us, we also would like to let the poor soul atone for his sins and for his attempts to meddle in domains into which man was never meant to pry.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Basically, I think when you tell him we have a werewolf- free place for him here on the island, I'm sure he'll agree to come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I cant say I agree with this...but I do appreciate that he's likely the only person that knows his way around the inside of someone's skull.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: You have my word that he will not be able to continue his depraved experiments here on this island and will instead be moulded into a useful and faithful follower of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Tell him that he is welcome here in exchange for his help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not at the moment, thanks.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Are they here yet? I can hear them...howling!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For all the things you've done, I'd like to pour barbecue sauce on your head and pitch you out that window.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Instead, I've come to offer you a way out of this castle.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Anything, anything. I will do anything to escape the wolves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you perform multiple brain surgeries on a number of zombie monks?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you heard me. A number of monks on an island off the coast of Mos Le'Harmless have had their brains placed in zombie pirate bodies, and vice versa, and they want you to switch them back.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I see...and how long have you been hearing these 'monks' talking to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hands up, everyone who is being hunted by packs of ravenous killer werewolves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, why, that would be you, doctor.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Alright, alright! Whatever you say!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I mean, you have to admit that this is a little...unlikely a scenario.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, I'm sure that a man that stitches corpses together for a living would be a good judge of rationality.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Did you come here to ask me something or just to insult me?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: And my medical practice was my living. The corpses thing was just a hobby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I came here to tell you that a group of monks need your services, and that they are willing to shelter you from the werewolves if you will use your medical skills to help them.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Hmm, sounds like an offer I'd have to be really mad to refuse...

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I accept, but you'll have to get me past those werewolves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you've lived here long enough. Is there some way I can smuggle you past?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Hmm, their sense of smell is quite acute, so no disguise would work. And I have a slight...reaction...to teleporting.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Wait... Rufus might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, he occasionally delivers meat to clients if they are too far away to come and get it themselves. If I were inside a crate of meat then he'd be unable to smell me and you'd be able to get me past.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, that seems sensible. I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rufus, can I have a wolf whistle?

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Well, I have plenty, but you seem to have no space for it. Come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Surrre! Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rufus, can I have some crate parts?

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Well surrre, as soon as you have some frrree space for them.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Yes, herrre are some new ones.

sections.title: Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain before building the crate

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: So how is the plan progressing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to talk to Rufus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have got Rufus to agree to let me ship some meat, but I have to build the crate and fill it with fake cats first.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Cats? He thought you were shipping cats?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, I don't know...if you were a werewolf, I can see you would strike him as THAT sort of person.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Wait? What sort of person?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Oh, it's nothing to worry about.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Hahahahahahahahahaha! Cats...it all makes sense...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I get the feeling I'm being maligned...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Rufus, I heard that you do deliveries of meat. Could you send me a crate of supplies to Mos Le'Harmless?

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Well, it's a bit of a way to go, but for you, I'd wrrrite up a shipping orderrr.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: What do you want to send?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, some meat?

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Well, I didn't think you wanted to send shoes, frrriend; this is a meat shop. What I mean is, what KIND of meat do you want to send?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, don't worry about it, I'll be providing the meat myself. It's a treat for some werewolves down there, you see.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Hmm, I hadn't hearrrd about any packs running with the pirrrates...still, I trust you.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Look, I apprrreciate you'rrre wanting to catch the meat yourrrself, I mean that's the sort of wolf I am too, but therrre is one thing.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: I'll gladly give you a crate for the meat, but I'll have to inspect it beforrre you send it over. I don't want my name associated with anything less than 100% frrresh, you understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh sure, that's entirely reasonable.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Grrreat! I have the parrrts for the crrrate forrr you when you have the space to carrrry them.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Grrreat! Here's the parrrts of the crrrate. Sorrrry, but I seem to be out of nails.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: So, can I get a hint as to what you arrre sending?

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Rrrabbits? Ducks? Sheep?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm sure that I'll be able to find some good meat from somewhere.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Wait, you are sending it live, rrright?

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: I mean it is going to take a while to get therrre, so I'm not going to ship it unless it's at least a little alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, I mean, what do you mean only a little alive? Of course a good werewolf like me will be sending the food live.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Ha, ha, ha! Of courrrse, of course!

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: So, what can I expect to see when I inspect this shipment of yourrrs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr, you know, something...special...

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: It's cats isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cats?

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Oh, don't worrrry, you sly dog, I'll not tell anyone.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Tell you what, I'll get the shipping order for a crrrate of cats wrrritten up rrright now. You just provide the meat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: No buts, frrriend, you get going. I know how trrricky those things are to grab!

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: You're carrying too much for me to give this to you now, but I have a whistle you can blow to call me when you have the crrrate filled.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Come talk to me when you have some frrree space forrr it.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Here, when you need me to inspect the meat, give this a blow and we'll get those tasty kitties of yours shipped off lickety split!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, at least I got him to agree to ship the crate for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose in the meantime I'll set the crate up in Fenkenstrain's tower where he can get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All I have to do now is get a load of cats from somewhere...

lines.speaker: Elfinlocks
lines.text: Keep up, cat!

lines.speaker: Clockwork cat
lines.text: Click, whirr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! I'll make some fake cats from wood and fur!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With a little help from the Ring of Charos, he'll never know the difference.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's just crazy enough to work...

sections.title: Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain before building the false bottom

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, that's a nice crate, but unless you want me to sit under a layer of (heh heh) cats, I think it'll need a false bottom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. And if you keep up these cat remarks I'm going to make sure the crate is shipped upside down, and not give you any padding.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Fine. *cough* Catlover *cough*.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't make me hurt you!

sections.title: Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain after building the false bottom

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, it'll be a tight fit, but I'm sure I'll be able to squeeze in.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Now all you have to do is go and get all your little kitty friends!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Be quiet or I'm going to post this thing direct to Canifis!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is with this whole cat thing anyway?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Nothing you need to worry about.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Hahaha - nothing - hehehehe - at all.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Mwuhahahahahahaha! Oh, it's so apt, I can't stop laughing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That does it, I'm leaving some nails in the bottom of the crate for him to sit on...

sections.title: Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain after filling up the crate with mechanical cats

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, you certainly seem to know what you are doing when it comes to rounding up cats. You must get a lot of practice!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urge to kill...rising!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, regardless, everything seems to be ready for you to call Rufus.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Blow your whistle and I'll get in the crate while we wait.

sections.title: Smuggling Dr Fenkenstrain out of the castle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, Doctor, time to get in the crate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's hope this works...

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Well, frrriend, I take it you have-

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: What are you doing herrre of all places? Don't you smell that weedy human, Fenkenstrrrain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well yeah, but this was the only place I could think of to store the cats.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Well, I know I wouldn't want anyone to find out I had a habit like yourrrs, but still...

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: I take it this is the crrrate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That it is!

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: You know, they look verrry still...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They must be pining.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Pining?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Pining.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Seems rrreasonable. Is that why they arrren't making any noise?

lines.speaker: Box of cats
lines.text: Err...meow?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See, they made a noise.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: You know, I think therrre might be...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing wrong whatsoever.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: ...nothing wrrrrong whatsoever!

lines.speaker: Box of cats
lines.text: Meow?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think the meowing point has been more than made.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Herrre is the shipping orderrr. Just paste it to send that crrrate on its way!

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: Although I'd prefer it if you took yourrr cat habit elsewherrre in futurrre. You understand, rrright?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't, but I'll promise you this is the last time I do this.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: That's good. You should rrreally kick the habit beforrre it gets too late.

lines.speaker: Rufus
lines.text: See you laterrr, frrriend!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, Rufus, see you around!

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain in a box
lines.text: Has he gone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, he's gone.

lines.speaker: Fenkenstrain in a box
lines.text: I liked the part where he asked you to keep your cat habit to yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bear in mind I can still post you to Canifis if I choose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gah...let's get this over with...

sections.title: Sending Dr Fenkenstrain to Harmony Island

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It teleported...but didn't he say he was allergic...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear, I do hope the doctor's reaction is not too violent.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: The doctor was delivered safely, although it seems like a bunch of pirates have stolen your cats.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Would you like me to take you to Harmony?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Well, the doctor is downstairs, waiting for you. I think he said he needed some things before he could begin.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Want me to take you back to Mos Le'Harmless?

sections.title: Talking to Dr Fenkenstrain at Harmony Island

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: When you have all the equipment I asked for, come and see me so we can begin the procedure.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: You teleported me! Twice!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was entirely unaware of at least one of those teleports.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I told you I reacted badly to teleportation!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, on the plus side, there was plenty of water to clean my clothing with afterwards...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Too...much...information! Mind...reeling!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Aside from that, I seem to have arrived in one piece. I apologise for not believing you earlier. Brother Tranquility has told me the full story and I am prepared to help.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: However, I am going to need a few things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, what do you need?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I will need a cranial clamp, a pair of brain tongs, three bell jars and at least thirty skull staples.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Oh, and a hammer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brain tongs? Skull staples?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you suggest I get these things, 'Unusual Implements R Us'?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, the zombies holed up in the main monastery must have at least one set of those items; otherwise, how did they perform the operation in the first place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A fair point. I'll pop back through that secret passage and ambush them.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: That won't work. Apparently they have sealed off that passage. The monks heard some explosions and saw the zombies moving something off the ship.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: It seems they found the passage as they did a little interior rearranging.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then, it looks like I'll go in through the front door.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Hah, only if you can walk through wood! They sealed it with planks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, that does it. I'll...I'll...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait a moment... That ship has cannons, right?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I believe so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I'll storm on board, grab some gunpowder and blow those doors off their hinges!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: That sounds pretty dangerous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, this is nothing. All in a day's work for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you later, doctor. Whatever party they are planning in there, I have to go pick up my invitation.

sections.title: Retrieving the items for Dr Fenkenstrain
Retrieving the items for Dr Fenkenstrain

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a hammer for you, doctor.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Very nice. Although, a sterilised one would be better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some skull staples for you, doctor.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Hmmm, size 3 staples. Interesting...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a cranial clamp for you, doctor.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Excellent, this will come in handy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a cranial clamp for you, doctor.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Wonderful...and they even have easy-grip handles, too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a bell jar for you, doctor.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: It's exactly this style of jar that I need, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some bell jars for you, doctor.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: It's exactly this style of jar that I need, thank you.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: When you have all the equipment I asked for, come and see me so we can begin the procedure.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, that's all of the items I'll need to get this situation fixed up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we can begin immediately?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Could you please keep notes of what I do, for posterity?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Certainly. That and I'll be keeping an eye on you to make sure nothing 'untoward' happens to those brains.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, that is your prerogative.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Let us get this operation started. I'll begin by using the...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want my eyes anymore!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Pah, you have no stomach for science.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'm going to be sick...

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Well, if you are, do it in a bucket. This is a sterile environment, after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But, but, it fell on the floor!

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Only a little, and the sawdust scraped right off with that cheese grater.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Now, why not follow them upstairs while I clean up.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I'm sure as soon as their headaches fade, they will want to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things going, doctor?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I have been talking with some of the monks. They do seem to be rather intelligent for members of the clergy.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: The ones with brains, that is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure that glowing review will endear you to them.

sections.title: Giving Brother Tranquility the good news

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Owwwww...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's the...brain?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Hurts...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guessed. Look, I'm going to head out and kill this Mi- Gor joker before he can get his crew together and sail away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You going to be okay?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Yeah...wow...spinny room...

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: W-wait...I have a bad feeling about this...

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: The pirates m-must have moved something large into the s-secret room to b-block it, and Mi-Gor mentioned he had a bodyguard.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: You should be prepared for a f-fierce battle.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Have a word with the others. They will bless you before you leave.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: D-do you want me to take you back to the island?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Would you like to receive a blessing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Well...ow...at least...oof...I'm back in the right body...

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: So...woozy...

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Ow, ow...those staples sting a little...

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: This is the worst hangover ever...and I didn't even get drunk!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll come back when you feel a little better.

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze!* You! I will peel you like a grape for your interference!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's pretty big talk for someone who can't even breathe properly!

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze!* Filthy little insect! Wretched little vermin! Let us see how witty you feel after my barrel-chested bodyguard has beaten you to paste!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hah, I don't care how muscular your bodyguard is. I'll still defeat them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What...what is that?

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze!* Hekhekhekhek... 'That' is my bodyguard, the Barrelchest!

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze!* I was going to test his capabilities by letting him wipe out the monks, but I believe a little warm up is in order first.

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze!* Barrelchest - Order: Annihilate that [man/woman]!

lines.speaker: Mi-Gor
lines.text: *Wheeze* And now I will take my leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you'll not have to worry about Mi-Gor any more. I defeated his creation and he seems to have slipped away.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Yes, one of the brothers observed him and a few of his sorebones slipping into the water shortly after there was a loud bang from inside the building.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: It is a pity he escaped, but Saradomin's justice will find him in the end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One thing does strike me as odd. He mentioned the name Rabid Jack, which I have heard in connection to zombie pirates before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me who that is?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: I cannot, but I do recall hearing it before.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: One of the pirates on the island said it while in the grip of a terrible fever. The others quietened him down and refused to answer any questions about it.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: I have no doubt that they know who it is, but I get the feeling that they will not tell you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...I'm sure I might be able to get them to say if I persuade them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still, that's a job for another time. After all that work, I think I might take a bit of a rest.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Certainly! Saradomin blesses you for your valiant effort!

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Here is a lamp blessed by Saradomin. If you pray over it, it will grant you wisdom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear that?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Hear what?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Oh, that? Sounds a little like locusts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, time for me to be off, I think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's your head feeling?

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: A lot better, thanks. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really. I was just popping by to see how you were all getting on and possibly help cull some of the zombies.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Your concern is most gratifying. Saradomin be with you.

lines.speaker: Brother Tranquility
lines.text: Do you want me to take you back to Mos Le'Harmless?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things going, doctor?

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: After talking with these monks and seeing your efforts to save them...I'm starting to regret my past actions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, we all make mistakes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Admittedly, most of us don't accidentally trip and sew a bunch of dead bodies together.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: I know...I think that my time here will be well spent trying to atone for what I did.

lines.speaker: Dr Fenkenstrain
lines.text: Thank you for helping me see the error of my ways, [player name].
